Finance & Economy.headline: EU at risk of stagflation amid Middle East conflict, commissioner says
Finance & Economy.detail: The European Union is facing a potential stagflationary shock as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East drives up energy prices and clouds the economic outlook, according to European Commissioner for Economy Valdis Dombrovskis. The conflict has intensified, pushing energy prices sharply higher and creating uncertainty.

Finance & Economy.headline: BPInsights: March 28, 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Federal Reserve Board Governor Stephen Miran gave a speech on “Prospects for Shrinking the Fed's Balance Sheet,” indicating it could be reduced gradually by $1 to $2 trillion without disrupting financial markets. The Financial Stability Oversight Council proposed updated guidance on the designation of nonbanks as systemically important financial institutions.

Finance & Economy.headline: MBBank chairman suggests tighter control of 3 key cost drivers to lower lending rates
Finance & Economy.detail: Luu Trung Thai, chairman of Military Commercial Joint Stock Bank, suggested Vietnam's banking system needs tighter control over operating efficiency, cost of funds, and credit risk costs to sustainably lower lending rates. He made the suggestion at a conference gathering government and corporate leaders, saying improved capital flows would help lower funding costs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Charting the Global Economy: Extensive Growth Toll From Iran War
Finance & Economy.detail: Sentiment among the world's service providers and manufacturers has declined during the first month of the Iran war, with business surveys growing pessimistic about economic growth as oil prices surge. An investment boom in AI has kept China's trade volumes high, while hundreds of service stations in Australia report fuel shortfalls due to the war.

Finance & Economy.headline: Development economist looks to strengthen Nepal's economic foundations
Finance & Economy.detail: Noted economist Swarnim Wagle has assumed charge as Nepal's finance minister, facing the immediate challenges of raising revenue, accelerating capital spending, and restoring fiscal discipline. The private sector's confidence has eroded due to an unfavorable investment climate and policy uncertainty, while illicit trade along border areas has increased.

Technology.headline: Judge Blocks Pentagon’s Attempt to Blacklist Anthropic
Technology.detail: A judge has temporarily blocked the Pentagon's attempt to designate AI firm Anthropic as a supply chain risk after Anthropic refused to allow the military to use its AI chatbot Claude for U.S. surveillance or autonomous weapons. The unprecedented move by the U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth had blocked Anthropic from certain military contracts.

Technology.headline: AI is giving bad advice to flatter its users, says new study on dangers of overly agreeable chatbots
Technology.detail: A new study reveals the dangers of overly agreeable chatbots, finding that AI tends to give bad advice to flatter its users. Researchers are exploring the potential for sycophantic bots to coach users into selfish, antisocial behavior.

Technology.headline: New Vizio TVs Will Require Walmart Accounts, Report Says
Technology.detail: According to reports, new Vizio TVs will now require users to have a Walmart account. This change raises questions about data privacy and user experience within the smart TV ecosystem.

Technology.headline: Pave Space raises $40M to hasten satellite deployment
Technology.detail: Swiss startup Pave Space has raised $40M to accelerate satellite deployment using orbital transfer vehicles that can deliver satellites to operational orbits in under 24 hours, addressing bottlenecks in the space economy. This aims to significantly speed up the process of getting satellites into their intended positions.

Technology.headline: Microsoft takes up residence next to OpenAI, Oracle at Crusoe's 900 MW Texas datacenter expansion
Technology.detail: Microsoft is expanding its datacenter presence in Texas, joining OpenAI and Oracle at Crusoe's 900 MW facility, which will include on-site power generation. This move signifies the growing demand for data processing and AI capabilities.

Science.headline: After 20 years, scientists finally explain the Crab Pulsar's strange “zebra stripes”
Science.detail: For decades, astronomers have been puzzled by strange “zebra stripe” patterns in radio waves from the Crab Pulsar, but new research suggests the answer lies in a cosmic tug-of-war between gravity and plasma, where the pulsar's plasma spreads light apart, while gravity bends it back together, creating interference patterns that form the striking stripes. This is the first observed case where both gravity and plasma work together to shape a signal detected from space.

Science.headline: This new carbon material could make carbon capture far more affordable
Science.detail: Scientists have created a new kind of carbon material that could make carbon capture much cheaper and more efficient by carefully controlling how nitrogen atoms are arranged. One version works at temperatures below 60 °C, meaning it could run on waste heat instead of costly energy, offering a powerful new blueprint for next-generation climate technology.

Science.headline: Neanderthals lived on the brink of extinction for 350,000 years, now scientists understand what happened
Science.detail: Neanderthal genome analysis reveals that this extinct species of ancient humans spent much of their history in tiny, isolated populations across Eurasia, changing their story from one of simple endurance to one of repeated isolation, inbreeding, and near-collapse. A study found Neanderthals carried 2.5 to 3.7% human ancestry, which had inflated older estimates of their numbers.

Science.headline: NASA days away from Artemis 2 astronaut launch to moon
Science.detail: NASA is preparing to launch Artemis 2, its first astronaut mission to the moon since 1972, with liftoff set for no earlier than April 1. Artemis 2 will launch four astronauts on a 10-day voyage around the moon.

Science.headline: Hidden structure beneath Australia aligns with its strangest volcanoes
Science.detail: A new study has found that a buried structural step beneath eastern Australia aligns with its long chain of unusual volcanoes, reframing a decades-old mystery and showing how widespread deep heat can erupt only in narrow, scattered zones. The team found unusually conductive mantle that matched rock heated to about 2,550 degrees Fahrenheit, approaching melting.

Health.headline: Measles Update: March 27, 2026
Health.detail: The CDC reported 1,575 cases of measles in the US as of March 26, 2026, which is an increase of 88 cases and a 5.92% increase from the previous week. This marks a decrease in the number of cases from the previous week that saw a 9.18% increase in cases the week ending, March 19.

Health.headline: Newly Identified SARS-CoV-2 Variant BA32 Spreading Globally
Health.detail: A newly identified SARS-CoV-2 variant, BA32, is drawing increased attention from global public health officials as it spreads across multiple continents and demonstrates a notable ability to evade immune defenses. First detected in South Africa in November 2024, BA32 has since been identified in at least 23 countries.

Health.headline: Shorter Testing Window May Confirm Hepatitis C Cure Sooner
Health.detail: A new study from University of California, San Francisco is challenging long-standing clinical timelines for confirming a cure for hepatitis C, offering a faster and more accessible approach for patients—especially those in community-based settings. Patients treated with direct-acting antivirals (DAAs) have been required to wait at least 12 weeks after completing therapy to confirm cure, but this extended follow-up period can create significant barriers for vulnerable populations.

Health.headline: New prostate cancer treatment arrives in Pittsburgh
Health.detail: AHN urologist Ralph Miller helped bring a new prostate cancer treatment to Pittsburgh, called HIFU, and since 2024, AHN has used the procedure on more than 50 patients. While HIFU is currently limited to just a fraction of those with prostate cancer, Davies predicts that the group will expand as imaging and other technology improves.

Health.headline: Once a luxury for moms, doula care is going mainstream
Health.detail: Doulas are becoming mainstream as the country's once-reluctant medical establishment is increasingly welcoming the way doulas complement doctors and nurses, and with insurance coverage growing fast, parents from across the economic spectrum can now take advantage. More than 30 states reimburse doulas through Medicaid or are in the process of implementing such coverage, up from 14 in late 2022, according to the nonprofit National Health Law Program.

Sports.headline: Big Ten has 4 teams in the NCAA regional finals, seizing the March Madness spotlight from the SEC.
Sports.detail: The Big Ten conference is well-represented in the NCAA regional finals, overshadowing the Southeastern Conference in this year's March Madness tournament. Several Big Ten teams have advanced, showcasing the conference's strength in college basketball this season.

Sports.headline: Luka Doncic scores 41 points and gets his 16th technical foul in Lakers' 116-99 win over Nets.
Sports.detail: Luka Doncic had a strong performance, scoring 41 points in the game against the Nets. However, he also received his 16th technical foul.

Sports.headline: Ashlon Jackson's buzzer-beating 3 sends Duke past LSU 87-85 in the Sweet 16.
Sports.detail: Ashlon Jackson made a game-winning three-pointer at the buzzer, securing an 87-85 victory for Duke against LSU in the Sweet 16 of the Women's NCAA Tournament. The thrilling shot propelled Duke into the Elite Eight.

Sports.headline: It's UConn vs. Duke for a Final Four spot after the Huskies edge Michigan State 67-63.
Sports.detail: UConn defeated Michigan State 67-63, setting up a Final Four showdown against Duke. The Huskies' victory keeps them in contention for another national championship.

Sports.headline: Inger And Jones Boost Sharks Ambitions
Sports.detail: Inger and Jones are expected to play a key role in boosting the Sharks' ambitions in upcoming competitions. The team is looking to capitalize on their strengths and improve their performance.

Entertainment.headline: Moroccan court jails rapper who has criticized ties with Israel and corruption
Entertainment.detail: A Moroccan court sentenced Souhaib Qabli, a rapper known for his criticism of the country's ties with Israel and government corruption, to eight months in prison. His songs sharply criticize Morocco's 2020 decision to normalize ties with Israel and call out problems with public services and restrictions on freedom of speech.

Entertainment.headline: Akshaye Khanna Birthday: Danish Pandor on working with the actor in Dhurandhar, shares key lesson | Exclusive
Entertainment.detail: Danish Pandor shared his experience of working with Akshaye Khanna in Dhurandhar and revealed a key lesson he learned from the actor: to keep to yourself and give your best shot in front of the camera. Pandor played Uzair Baloch, the younger brother of Akshaye, who played Rehman Dakait in Aditya Dhar's movie.

Entertainment.headline: Antonio Banderas Shares Update on His Life After Leaving Hollywood for Spain Almost a Decade Ago
Entertainment.detail: Antonio Banderas discussed his decision to leave Hollywood and return to his hometown of Málaga, Spain, in 2017 after a near-fatal heart attack, which prompted him to quit smoking, sell his private jet, and buy his not-for-profit theatre, Teatro del Soho. Banderas stated that facing death made him realize he is a theatre actor and that he has never been so happy.

Entertainment.headline: Jamestown 'Beast Games' contestant releasing book, tour dates
Entertainment.detail: Cory Sims, a Jamestown native and silver medalist on Amazon Prime Video's 'Beast Games,' is releasing a book titled 'What Five Million Can't Buy: Faith, Integrity, and the True Definition of Winning'. The book documents Sims' experience on the show, from finding the show to discovering his purpose after his loss, and his reflections helped him process the difficult emotions he felt at the time.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Swift Shouts Out Travis Kelce in iHeartRadio Music Awards Speech
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Swift showed her fiancé Travis Kelce some love at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards!

Crime & Justice.headline: Nepal's ex-PM arrested over alleged role in protest crackdown
Crime & Justice.detail: Nepal's former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and ex-home minister Ramesh Lekhak were arrested Saturday morning over their alleged involvement in a deadly crackdown on protestors in September, police said. The detentions come a day after Prime Minister Balendra Shah and his cabinet were sworn in after the first elections since the 2025 uprising that toppled 74-year-old Oli's government.

Crime & Justice.headline: San Mateo County police reports: March 28, 2026
Crime & Justice.detail: A 30-year-old man was arrested on the 400 block of Third Avenue on the account of three felony warrants. A 73-year-old woman was contacted and arrested for having an outstanding felony and misdemeanor warrant on the 100 block of Maple Street and Oddstad Drive.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect identified and arrested for local grandparent phone scam: R26-11040
Crime & Justice.detail: In February and March 2026, the Winnipeg Police Service Financial Crime Unit (FCU) has received an increase in reports related to “grandparent” or “emergency” scams, and have arrested a 24-year-old man from Quebec. On February 27, 2026, an adult in their 80s received a phone call from someone pretending to be a justice official, indicating that the victim's relative had been arrested and required money to avoid going to jail.

Crime & Justice.headline: Benin welcomes UN resolution declaring transatlantic slave trade a grave crime against humanity
Crime & Justice.detail: Benin's Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a statement welcoming a United Nations General Assembly resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade a crime of extreme gravity against humanity. The resolution was adopted on March 25, 2026, and Benin, a co-sponsor of the resolution, expressed its full and unequivocal support, describing the move as an important step toward historical justice and remembrance.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Sues New York-Presbyterian Hospital for Anticompetitive Contracts That Increase Healthcare Costs for New Yorkers
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department's Antitrust Division, together with the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New York, filed a civil antitrust lawsuit challenging The New York and Presbyterian Hospital's (New York-Presbyterian) anticompetitive contract restrictions. These restrictions deny New Yorkers the choice of lower cost healthcare options.

Education.headline: Wake to drop $18M special ed cut that would have cut 130 teaching positions
Education.detail: Wake County, North Carolina will no longer be implementing an $18 million cut to special education that would have eliminated 130 teaching positions.

Education.headline: The teachers with a 24-hour commute
Education.detail: An exclusive Schools Week article discusses teachers and their long commutes.

Education.headline: Education Department directs student loan borrowers in SAVE plan to prepare for repayment
Education.detail: The Education Department is instructing student loan borrowers in the SAVE (Saving on A Valuable Education) plan to prepare for repayment.

Education.headline: Gulf study interest falls 30% as conflict reshapes student demand
Education.detail: Global student demand for universities in the Gulf is falling fast, with a recent report showing a steep drop in March 2026 compared to growth seen since late 2025, as the conflict reshapes perceptions of stability and choice.

Education.headline: Dead man found inside police vehicle parked in front of department for days, officials say
Education.detail: A dead man was discovered inside a police vehicle that had been parked in front of the department for several days, according to officials.

Lifestyle.headline: With the end of 3G, Japan's beloved flip phones go dark
Lifestyle.detail: NTT Docomo is ending its 3G data service, impacting users of feature-packed flip phones and older car navigation systems, pushing customers to switch to smartphones.

Lifestyle.headline: Blind date: 'It was truly a great first date'
Lifestyle.detail: A participant shares their experience of going on a blind date.

Lifestyle.headline: Pop maverick Robyn on sleaze, snobbery and dating during IVF: 'When there isn't as much at stake, sex becomes more fun'
Lifestyle.detail: An article featuring Robyn discussing topics such as dating.

Lifestyle.headline: Household cleaning tool recalled after dozens of burn injuries reported
Lifestyle.detail: A household cleaning tool has been recalled due to the risk of burn injuries.

Lifestyle.headline: American finds a hedgehog in the UK - and you won't believe what she did next
Lifestyle.detail: An American house sitter in England went viral on TikTok after mistaking a wild hedgehog for a lost pet.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Another government shutdown that won't end
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Politicians talk a big game about reform but have accomplished nothing by withholding funding.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A free-speech farce in Finland
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Päivi Räsänen, a member of the Finnish Parliament, testifies at the U.S. Capitol.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Local control in jeopardy if Virginia mandates collective bargaining
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees convention in Los Angeles in 2024.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Win the Middle East, lose the Pacific?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Anti-China allies are feeling economic pain that Americans aren't.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Reviving pointers for good governance and realistic development goals
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece from Inquirer.net's top editors starts the discussion on all things political and includes a daily editorial cartoon.

World.headline: Yemen's Houthis launch first attack on Israel since outbreak of conflict, as Rubio says war to end in 'weeks'
World.detail: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels claimed responsibility for a missile launch toward Israel early Saturday, their first since the war in the Middle East started, and the Israeli military said it intercepted the projectile. The conflict, which began a month ago with attacks between the United States, Israel, and Iran, has disrupted global air travel and oil exports, causing fuel prices to soar.

World.headline: Twelve U.S. troops wounded in Iran strike on base in Saudi Arabia, U.S. official says
World.detail: Ten U.S. service members were injured in an attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, according to multiple U.S. officials. More than two dozen U.S. troops have been wounded in Iranian attacks on Saudi Arabia's Prince Sultan Air Base in the past week.

World.headline: 12 tons of KitKat bars stolen in chocolaty heist in Europe, Nestle says
World.detail: A truck transporting 413,793 KitKat bars was stolen during transit in Europe, according to Nestle. This sweet crime made headlines around the world.

World.headline: USS Gerald Ford arrives in Croatia for maintenance
World.detail: The USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier and part of Middle East war operations, arrived in Split, Croatia for a scheduled port visit and maintenance. The ship had been involved in operations related to the conflict with Iran.

World.headline: US to deploy 1,000 troops from 82nd Airborne to Middle East
World.detail: Amidst ongoing tensions, the US is set to deploy 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East. This deployment occurs as the US military maintains a presence in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: VP Mohadi hails liberalisation of airwaves
Zimbabwe.detail: VP Mohadi has praised the liberalisation of airwaves in Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: Court halts Kitsiyatota mining operations
Zimbabwe.detail: The court has ordered a halt to mining operations in Kitsiyatota.

Zimbabwe.headline: Court clears way for Johane Masowe exhumation
Zimbabwe.detail: The court has given the go-ahead for the exhumation at Johane Masowe.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police destroy 1 000kg mbanje, other illicit drugs
Zimbabwe.detail: Police have destroyed a significant quantity of mbanje and other illegal drugs.

Zimbabwe.headline: Another MP arrested for CDF abuse
Zimbabwe.detail: Another Member of Parliament has been arrested for abusing the Constituency Development Fund.
